headline: Micron stock jumps as investors look beyond GPUs in AI chip trade
summary: The artificial intelligence chip trade is not stopping at GPUs. Micron Technology (MU) shares rose on July 14 after KeyBanc raised its price target on the memory-chip maker, citing tighter supply and higher prices for DRAM, NAND and high-bandwidth memory, or HBM. Micron stock was recently trading ...

headline: Micron (MU) Stock May Look Reasonable Despite Fresh AI Expansion Plans
summary: Micron Technology stock has run very hard in recent years, yet the valuation checks send a more mixed signal. The question for investors is whether the current price still reflects reasonable expectations or is starting to run ahead of itself. Micron has delivered roughly 14x total return over the past 3 years, which puts extra pressure on today’s valuation to be backed by durable earnings power. Expectations around long term AI driven memory demand can support higher earnings assumptions,...

headline: AI Chips Update - Arteris Arm Partnership Strengthens AI Chip Security
summary: In a significant development for the AI chip sector, Arteris, Inc. has announced an expanded partnership with Arm to enhance semiconductor cybersecurity. This alliance builds on a five-year collaboration, utilizing Arteris' Cycuity Radix hardware security assurance product. By integrating Cycuity Radix into Arm's CPU design processes, the partnership aims to proactively address potential security vulnerabilities and strengthen the security assurance of processor cores. This strategic move...

headline: SK Hynix vs. Micron deep dive: who wins the AI memory supercycle?
summary: Investing.com -- SK Hynix's $26.5 billion Nasdaq debut on July 10, 2026—the largest U.S. listing ever by a foreign company and the second largest IPO in history—highlighted an obvious question: with AI investments breaking records and the HBM supercycle in full swing, does the Korean market-share leader, or its fierce U.S. competitor Micron, capture more of the upside?

content.title: SK Hynix vs. Micron deep dive: who wins the AI memory supercycle?
